Justification: The kettle shall have a capacity of 1.5 liters. Users will be educated on utilizing variable fill lines, particularly the minimum fill level, to ensure energy efficiency for smaller quantities, promoting responsible energy consumption.; This capacity is selected to optimize energy consumption per boil and reduce the overall material footprint while accommodating typical household needs.

The kettle shall boil 1.5 liters of water from ambient temperature (20°C) to boiling point (100°C) within approximately 6 minutes.; This target boiling time balances user satisfaction with energy efficiency, allowing for a lower wattage design and reduced overall energy consumption over the product's lifetime.; The boiling time for a minimum fill (0.5 liters) shall not exceed 3 minutes.

The kettle shall have a power rating of 1800W to provide a balance between boiling speed and energy efficiency over the product's lifetime.; This power rating ensures efficient operation without excessive strain on standard household electrical circuits. Efforts will be made to minimize total energy consumed per boil through design optimizations.

The exterior casing shall be constructed from 100% recycled stainless steel (e.g., 304 grade), sourced responsibly to ensure high recycled content and ethical manufacturing.; The recycled stainless steel will feature an integral, durable, easy-to-maintain finish that resists scratching and fading, and does not interfere with recycling processes. Coatings that hinder recycling are excluded.

The interior shall be made of 100% recycled stainless steel (e.g., 304 grade), ensuring food-grade safety and durability.; The heating element shall be a distinct, easily removable, and replaceable component made from durable, recyclable materials. Its connection to the interior will facilitate easy separation without creating composites that hinder recycling of either component. Welding or permanent bonding is excluded.

PDS Element: Use of Material / Number of Materials
Justification: The total number of distinct material types shall be minimized, prioritizing 100% recycled stainless steel for all primary components.; For non-contact, non-critical parts (e.g., wiring insulation), a durable, well-characterized, and easily recyclable polymer will be used. These polymers must be easily separable from stainless steel and have a clear recycling stream. Exotic or composite polymers are excluded.; All materials will be clearly labeled with standard recycling codes and meet food-grade and BPA-free requirements.
The kettle shall consume less than 0.5W in standby mode.; Heat retention of the kettle body will be optimized through material selection and design to reduce energy use during operation.

The auto shut-off mechanism shall be robust, reliable over the product's lifespan, and precise to prevent over-boiling and energy waste.; The mechanism will activate when boiling point is reached or when the kettle is lifted from its base.

A robust and reliable boil-dry protection mechanism shall be implemented, tested for longevity (minimum 3,000 cycles) to prevent premature product failure.; This feature automatically shuts off the heating element if no water is detected.

The kettle shall be designed for a minimum lifespan of 3,000 boiling cycles, achieved through robust component selection (thermostat, switch, heating element), high-quality materials, and design considerations for wear and tear.; Critical spare parts, including the heating element, thermostat, and switch, shall be available for at least 7-10 years to facilitate repair and extend product life. These parts will be designed for ease of replacement.

The kettle shall withstand a 0.5-meter drop onto a hard surface without catastrophic failure (e.g., shattering, loss of structural integrity, or function).; The handle, base, and lid hinge will be designed for impact resistance, prioritizing ease of disassembly and repair over extreme impact resistance if it compromises circularity. Damage from a drop must not prevent disassembly or material separation.

The handle shall be ergonomic, heat-resistant, and non-slip, constructed from recycled stainless steel with an integrated durable grip material.; The grip material will be easily separable from the stainless steel or compatible with stainless steel recycling. Separate insulation layers will be avoided.
The spout shall be drip-free and designed for easy manual cleaning to prevent limescale buildup and maintain hygiene.; The connection to the interior shall be smooth and accessible for cleaning.

The lid opening mechanism and hinge must be robust and durable for thousands of operations, designed to prevent premature failure using high-quality, separable materials.; Design considerations will focus on high-wear components to ensure longevity and ease of disassembly.

The interior shall be designed for easy manual cleaning and descaling, with accessible surfaces and heating element.; Materials shall resist limescale buildup and staining. Components will not be designated as dishwasher-safe if it compromises durability or disassembly. Avoidance of permanent adhesives in interior assembly.

The water level indicator shall be made from a durable material that resists fogging and discoloration over time, remaining visible and accurate throughout the product's life.; Crucially, it must be mechanically fastened or easily removable for cleaning, replacement, and recycling, without creating a composite with the stainless steel interior. It will be protected from direct steam.

Cord storage shall be designed to be durable and not put undue stress on the power cord or its connection point to the base, which are common failure points.; The mechanism will allow for neat storage and easy access, facilitating disassembly at end-of-life.

The target noise level shall be 60 dB during boiling, with noise reduction methods not compromising durability, repairability, or the use of sustainable materials.; Noise consistency throughout the boiling cycle will be confirmed. Noise reduction strategies will not rely on materials that hinder recycling or disassembly.

The kettle shall feature durable, easy-to-maintain finishes and materials that resist scratching, fading, and water spots to ensure the product maintains its appearance over its lifespan.; Design will prioritize a clean, minimalist appearance with functional integration of components, utilizing materials that align with sustainability goals.

Packaging shall utilize 100% recycled and recyclable materials, minimizing volume and weight.; Plastic tapes/coatings that hinder recyclability will be avoided, using water-based adhesives where possible. Packaging will be efficient for automated packing and provide adequate protection.

The kettle shall be designed for disassembly using mechanical fasteners (screws, clips) wherever possible, avoiding adhesives and composite materials that cannot be easily separated.; All materials will be clearly labeled with standard recycling codes. The heating element will be a distinct, easily removable component. Disassembly will not require specialized tools, and a simple disassembly guide will be provided.

The kettle shall meet relevant safety and energy efficiency certifications, including Energy Star (aiming for the highest efficiency tiers).; Certifications related to material sustainability (e.g., recycled content verification) and ethical manufacturing across the entire product lifecycle will be pursued to provide robust third-party validation.

A durable LED with a long lifespan shall be specified. Its failure must not compromise product function or safety.; The design will ensure easy replacement of the LED module if possible, without extensive disassembly or specialized tools. Its longevity will be exceptionally high and guaranteed.

The base shall be stable and non-slip with durable electrical contacts, designed for disassembly to allow separation of electronic components from structural materials for recycling.; The connection between the kettle and base will be robust for daily use and easily separable at EOL. Electrical contacts will be designed for longevity and ease of replacement if necessary.

Standard screws, common electrical connectors, and off-the-shelf components (thermostats, heating elements) will be used where possible to reduce tooling costs, lead times, and inventory complexity, while also simplifying repair.

Parts will be designed for easy assembly with clear orientation, appropriate snap-fits, and chamfered edges, facilitating easy disassembly for repair and recycling. Simple integration and sealing of components like the water level indicator and cord storage will be ensured to prevent leaks and ensure longevity.

High-volume, repeatable, and cost-effective fabrication processes such as deep drawing, stamping, and injection molding will be selected. Processes will minimize post-processing, be compatible with recycled materials, and be optimized for minimal waste and energy.
